title: Cardano Blockchain Ecosystem Budget: Amaru Node Development 2025

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: evidence did not clear the directional decision threshold. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide mitigation evidence; Provide independent assurance. Residual uncertainty: Rule-based system; does not infer unstated intent. Confidence context: score 0.01; confidence 57.2%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano Blockchain Ecosystem Budget: Amaru Node Development 2025
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano., It aims to improve the network's overall accessibility and robustness without compromising its safety and security., Amaru provides another perspective and solution for stake pool operators and developers alike, prioritizing a modular approach, a seamless user experience, and low hardware requirements., Additionally, the project is implemented mainly in Rust, which is well-equipped for high assurance to attract new contributors to the core maintenance of the ecosystem.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano.
Claim (other, independently_verifiable, high materiality): It aims to improve the network's overall accessibility and robustness without compromising its safety and security.

Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Amaru provides another perspective and solution for stake pool operators and developers alike, prioritizing a modular approach, a seamless user experience, and low hardware requirements.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Additionally, the project is implemented mainly in Rust, which is well-equipped for high assurance to attract new contributors to the core maintenance of the ecosystem.

Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Who we are Amaru is a multi-entity effort that currently sits under PRAGMA: a member-based, not-for-profit open-source association for blockchain software projects.

Claim (other, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Five members with equal power currently drive PRAGMA's strategy and operations: Blink Labs, Cardano Foundation, dcSpark, Sundae Labs, and TxPipe.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Amaru is an open-source project implementing a new fully interoperable block-producing node for Cardano." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of InfoAction.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
